title: Gas laws: PV = nRT and the combined gas law
blurb: The ideal gas law ties a gas's pressure, volume, amount, and temperature into one equation. These drills give you three of the four and ask for the fourth â€” and a second set walks a fixed sample from one state to another with the combined gas law. Type your answer (rounded to three significant figures); if you slip, the drill names the mistake â€” most often forgetting to convert آ°C to kelvin, or reaching for R = 8.314 when a pressure-in-atm problem wants R = 0.08206. The arithmetic is machine-checked; the ideal-gas model is disclosed, not proved.
skills: solve PV = nRT for any single variable
always convert temperature to kelvin (K = آ°C + 273.15)
pick the right R for the units: 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K for atm and litres
use Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚ for a fixed sample changing state

problems.prompt: An ideal gas sample has 0.5 atm, 7.39 L, 0.1 mol. Using PV = nRT (R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K), what is the temperature (K)?

diagnostics.misconception: Used R = 8.314 (the SI value in J/molآ·K) â€” for pressure in atm and volume in L, use R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K.

problems.explain: PV = nRT rearranges to T = PV / (nR). Substituting gives 450 K.

problems.prompt: A fixed sample of gas starts at 0.5 atm, 43.1 L, 350 K. It changes to 3 atm, 8.21 L. Using Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚, what is the new temperature (K)?

diagnostics.misconception: Inverted the state ratio â€” Tâ‚‚ = Tâ‚پ آ· (Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚)/(Pâ‚پVâ‚پ), not its reciprocal.

problems.explain: Hold the amount fixed: Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚. Rearranged, Tâ‚‚ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚Tâ‚پ / (Pâ‚پVâ‚پ) = 400 K.

problems.prompt: An ideal gas sample has 1 atm, 23 L, 100 آ°C. Using PV = nRT (R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K), what is the amount (mol)?

diagnostics.misconception: Used R = 8.314 (the SI value in J/molآ·K) â€” for pressure in atm and volume in L, use R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K.
Used the Celsius temperature directly â€” PV=nRT needs ABSOLUTE (kelvin) temperature. Convert first: 100 آ°C + 273.15 = 373.15 K.

problems.explain: PV = nRT rearranges to n = PV / (RT). First put the temperature in kelvin (100 آ°C + 273.15 = 373.15 K). Substituting gives 0.751 mol.

problems.prompt: A fixed sample of gas starts at 1.25 atm, 28.6 L, 290 K. It changes to 2 atm, 18.5 L. Using Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚, what is the new temperature (K)?

diagnostics.misconception: Inverted the state ratio â€” Tâ‚‚ = Tâ‚پ آ· (Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚)/(Pâ‚پVâ‚پ), not its reciprocal.

problems.explain: Hold the amount fixed: Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚. Rearranged, Tâ‚‚ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚Tâ‚پ / (Pâ‚پVâ‚پ) = 300 K.

problems.prompt: An ideal gas sample has 1 atm, 0.1 mol, 450 K. Using PV = nRT (R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K), what is the volume (L)?

diagnostics.misconception: Used R = 8.314 (the SI value in J/molآ·K) â€” for pressure in atm and volume in L, use R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K.

problems.explain: PV = nRT rearranges to V = nRT / P. Substituting gives 3.69 L.

problems.prompt: A fixed sample of gas starts at 3 atm, 3.73 L, 273 K. It changes to 0.75 atm, 450 K. Using Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚, what is the new volume (L)?

diagnostics.misconception: Flipped a ratio â€” set up Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚ and solve Vâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پPâ‚‚).
Inverted the temperatures â€” Tâ‚‚ goes on top, Tâ‚پ on the bottom (Vâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚/(Tâ‚پPâ‚‚)).

problems.explain: Hold the amount fixed: Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚. Rearranged, Vâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پPâ‚‚) = 24.6 L.

problems.prompt: An ideal gas sample has 3 atm, 2 mol, 250 آ°C. Using PV = nRT (R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K), what is the volume (L)?

diagnostics.misconception: Used R = 8.314 (the SI value in J/molآ·K) â€” for pressure in atm and volume in L, use R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K.
Used the Celsius temperature directly â€” PV=nRT needs ABSOLUTE (kelvin) temperature. Convert first: 250 آ°C + 273.15 = 523.15 K.

problems.explain: PV = nRT rearranges to V = nRT / P. First put the temperature in kelvin (250 آ°C + 273.15 = 523.15 K). Substituting gives 28.6 L.

problems.prompt: A fixed sample of gas starts at 3 atm, 7.18 L, 350 K. It changes to 55.4 L, 450 K. Using Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚, what is the new pressure (atm)?

diagnostics.misconception: Flipped a ratio â€” Pâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پVâ‚‚), so Vâ‚‚ divides and Tâ‚‚ multiplies.

problems.explain: Hold the amount fixed: Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚. Rearranged, Pâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پVâ‚‚) = 0.5 atm.

problems.prompt: An ideal gas sample has 1.25 atm, 9.78 L, 0.5 mol. Using PV = nRT (R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K), what is the temperature (K)?

diagnostics.misconception: Used R = 8.314 (the SI value in J/molآ·K) â€” for pressure in atm and volume in L, use R = 0.08206 Lآ·atm/molآ·K.

problems.explain: PV = nRT rearranges to T = PV / (nR). Substituting gives 298 K.

problems.prompt: A fixed sample of gas starts at 2 atm, 9.23 L, 450 K. It changes to 36.9 L, 450 K. Using Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚, what is the new pressure (atm)?

diagnostics.misconception: Flipped a ratio â€” Pâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پVâ‚‚), so Vâ‚‚ divides and Tâ‚‚ multiplies.

problems.explain: Hold the amount fixed: Pâ‚پVâ‚پ/Tâ‚پ = Pâ‚‚Vâ‚‚/Tâ‚‚. Rearranged, Pâ‚‚ = Pâ‚پVâ‚پTâ‚‚ / (Tâ‚پVâ‚‚) = 0.5 atm.

assumptions.claim: The gas behaves ideally: PV = nRT holds exactly only for a dilute gas (low pressure, temperature well above condensation). A disclosed model, not a law of algebra.
